title: ranunculus fuscinus
intro: Ranunculus fuscinus, also known as the lesser celandine or fig buttercup, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of ranunculin and protoanemonin, compounds that have shown potential in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus fuscinus include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, and soothe skin irritations. Ranunculus fuscinus can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential skin irritation.
